title: Stable Bubble Sort with Missing Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a stable bubble sort algorithm. The algorithm should sort the array in ascending order if the input array is already sorted in descending order.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the sorted array in ascending order.
statement.notes: The algorithm should be stable, i.e., equal elements should remain in their original order.

examples.explanation: Already sorted in ascending order, so return the same array.
Sorted in ascending order since the input was sorted in descending order.

hints.text: First hint text - Implement the bubble sort algorithm in a way that it checks for already sorted input.

editorial.title: Stable Bubble Sort with Missing Swap
editorial.intuition: The algorithm should check for already sorted input and return the same array if it is sorted in ascending order. If the input is sorted in descending order, it should sort it in ascending order.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to implement a bubble sort algorithm without any optimization and then check for already sorted input. However, this would be inefficient for large inputs.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement the bubble sort algorithm with optimization and check for already sorted input. This can be done by keeping a flag to check if any swaps were made during a pass through the array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The algorithm is correct because it correctly handles already sorted input and sorts the array in ascending order if the input is sorted in descending order.
editorial.dryRun: Example run: Input array [4, 3, 2, 0], already sorted in ascending order. Output array remains [4, 3, 2, 0].
commonMistakes: Not checking for already sorted input
Not implementing the bubble sort algorithm correctly
